name: Celery Root Bisque with Peppered Croutons
recipeIngredient: 2 and a quarter pounds Celery root, peeled,
5 cups Chicken stock or canned broth
4 Thyme sprigs, fresh, or 1 teaspoon Dried Thyme, crumbled
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a heavy large non-aluminum pot over medium heat.
2. Add onion and celery, and sauté until slightly softened, about 5 minutes.
3. Add celery roots, potatoes, and parsnips; sauté another 5 minutes.
4. Add 3 cups of stock (or canned broth), bay leaf, thyme, white pepper, and coriander seeds.
5. Bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat, cover partially, and simmer until vegetables are very soft, about 25 minutes.
7. Discard the bay leaf and thyme sprigs.
8. Add spinach to the soup and cook until just wilted, stirring occasionally, about 2 minutes.
9. Process the soup in batches, returning it to the pot.
10. Stir in cream and remaining 2 cups of stock; simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
11. Season with salt and white pepper as needed.
12. (The bisque can be prepared up to one day ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Bring to a simmer before continuing.)
13. Ladle the soup into bowls. Sprinkle with croutons and serve.
Instructions for Peppered Croutons:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Cut bread into cubes.
3. Toss with 2 tablespoons butter, seasoned generously with black pepper.
4. Spread on a baking sheet; bake until crisp and golden brown, about 15 minutes.
5. Remove from the oven and serve immediately.
